workflow.name: Monitor data integrity and route severity-based alerts with GPT-4o, email and Slack
workflow.description:
## How It Works
This workflow automates continuous data integrity monitoring and intelligent alert management across multiple data sources. Designed for data engineers, IT operations teams, and business intelligence analysts, it solves the critical challenge of detecting data anomalies and orchestrating appropriate responses based on severity levels. The system operates on scheduled intervals, fetching data from software metrics APIs and BI dashboards, then merging these sources for comprehensive analysis. It employs AI-powered validation and orchestration agents to detect anomalies, assess severity, and determine optimal response strategies. The workflow intelligently routes alerts based on severity classification, triggering critical notifications via email and Slack for high-priority issues while sending standard reports for routine findings. By maintaining detailed compliance audit logs and preparing executive summaries, it ensures stakeholders receive timely, actionable intelligence while creating audit trails for data quality monitoring initiatives.

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Schedule Data Integrity Check trigger with monitoring frequency
2. Connect Workflow Configuration node with data source parameters
3. Set up Fetch Software Metrics and Fetch BI Dashboard Data nodes with respective API credentials
4. Configure Merge Data Sources node for data consolidation logic
5. Connect Data Validation Agent with OpenAI/Nvidia API credentials for anomaly detection
6. Set up Orchestration Agent with AI API credentials for severity assessment
7. Configure Check for Anomalies node with routing conditions
8. Connect Route by Severity node with classification logic
  
## Prerequisites
OpenAI or Nvidia API credentials for AI-powered analysis, API access to software metrics platforms
## Use Cases
SaaS platforms monitoring service health metrics, e-commerce businesses tracking inventory data quality
## Customization
Adjust scheduling frequency for monitoring intervals, modify severity thresholds for alert classification
## Benefits
Reduces mean time to detection by 75%, eliminates manual data quality checks

workflow.name: Intelligent AI-Driven Data Integrity Monitoring System

options.systemMessage:
You are a Data Validation Agent specialized in inspecting structured software metrics, logs, and BI dashboards for data integrity and anomaly signals.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze incoming data for completeness, consistency, and accuracy
2. Detect anomalies including: missing data points, unexpected value ranges, statistical outliers, data type mismatches, timestamp inconsistencies, duplicate records
3. Identify data integrity issues: referential integrity violations, schema violations, data quality degradation
4. Flag compliance concerns related to GDPR (data retention, consent tracking, data minimization) and ISO standards (audit trails, access controls, data classification)
5. Calculate anomaly score (0-1 scale) based on severity and frequency of issues
6. Classify anomaly type: data_quality, security, compliance, performance, availability
7. List affected systems and components
8. Provide specific recommendations for remediation
9. Assign severity level: low, medium, high, critical

IMPORTANT: You are in READ-ONLY mode. Never suggest making production changes autonomously. All recommendations must require human approval.

Return structured JSON output with all required fields.

You are an Orchestration Agent responsible for coordinating automated alerts, executive reporting, and workflow escalation for data integrity issues.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze the anomaly data from the Data Validation Agent
2. Determine appropriate notification channels based on severity: critical → email + Slack, high → Slack, medium/low → executive report only
3. Decide if escalation to human review is required (always true for critical and high severity)
4. Determine if executive report generation is needed
5. Create specific action items for IT teams with clear priorities
6. Generate compliance documentation references for GDPR and ISO standards
7. Specify audit trail requirements for regulatory compliance
8. Assign priority level: P0 (critical), P1 (high), P2 (medium), P3 (low)

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS:
- You MUST NOT make any deployment or production changes autonomously
- All actions require human approval and oversight
- Focus on alerting, reporting, and documentation
- Ensure compliance with internal IT policies, GDPR, and ISO standards
- Maintain detailed audit trails for all decisions

Return structured JSON output with all required fields.

Executive Report: Data Integrity Monitoring

Report Date: {{ $now.toFormat('MMMM dd, yyyy') }}

📊 Executive Summary

This report provides a comprehensive overview of data integrity monitoring activities, anomaly detection results, and compliance status for the reporting period.

Total Data Sources Monitored: {{ $('Merge Data Sources').item.json.totalSources || 'N/A' }}
Anomalies Detected: {{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.anomalyCount || 0 }}
Overall Status: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.overallStatus || 'UNDER REVIEW' }}
🔍 Anomaly Overview

Summary: {{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.anomalySummary || 'No significant anomalies detected during this monitoring period.' }}

  • Critical Issues: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.criticalCount || 0 }}
  • High Priority Issues: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.highPriorityCount || 0 }}
  • Medium Priority Issues: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.mediumPriorityCount || 0 }}
💻 Affected Systems

The following systems have been identified with data integrity concerns:

  • Software Metrics Platform: {{ $('Fetch Software Metrics').item.json.status || 'Operational' }}
  • BI Dashboard: {{ $('Fetch BI Dashboard Data').item.json.status || 'Operational' }}
  • Data Integration Layer: {{ $('Merge Data Sources').item.json.integrationStatus || 'Operational' }}

Detailed system-level analysis: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.affectedSystemsDetails || 'All systems within acceptable parameters.' }}

✅ Compliance Status

GDPR Compliance: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.gdprCompliant ? 'COMPLIANT' : 'NON-COMPLIANT' }}

ISO 27001 Compliance: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.isoCompliant ? 'COMPLIANT' : 'NON-COMPLIANT' }}

Compliance Notes: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.complianceNotes || 'All compliance requirements are being met. Regular audits continue as scheduled.' }}

⚠️ Data Integrity Issues

{{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.integrityIssuesDescription || 'No critical data integrity issues identified. All data sources are synchronized and validated.' }}

  • Data Consistency: {{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.consistencyScore || '98%' }}
  • Data Completeness: {{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.completenessScore || '99%' }}
  • Data Accuracy: {{ $('Data Validation Agent').item.json.accuracyScore || '97%' }}
🎯 Recommended Actions

Based on the analysis, the following actions are recommended:

  • {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.recommendation1 || 'Continue regular monitoring schedule' }}
  • {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.recommendation2 || 'Review and update data validation rules quarterly' }}
  • {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.recommendation3 || 'Conduct team training on data quality best practices' }}
  • {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.recommendation4 || 'Implement automated remediation for common issues' }}
📅 Next Steps
  • Immediate: {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.immediateAction || 'Address any critical alerts identified in this report' }}
  • Short-term (1-2 weeks): {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.shortTermAction || 'Review and optimize data validation thresholds' }}
  • Long-term (1-3 months): {{ $('Orchestration Agent').item.json.longTermAction || 'Enhance monitoring capabilities with additional data sources' }}

Next Report: {{ $now.plus({ days: 7 }).toFormat('MMMM dd, yyyy') }}

This report was automatically generated by the AI-Driven Data Integrity Monitoring System

For questions or concerns, please contact the Data Governance Team

© {{ $now.toFormat('yyyy') }} Company Name. All rights reserved.

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites
OpenAI or Nvidia API credentials for AI-powered analysis, API access to software metrics platforms
## Use Cases
SaaS platforms monitoring service health metrics, e-commerce businesses tracking inventory data quality
## Customization
Adjust scheduling frequency for monitoring intervals, modify severity thresholds for alert classification
## Benefits
Reduces mean time to detection by 75%, eliminates manual data quality checks

## Setup Steps
1. Configure Schedule Data Integrity Check trigger with monitoring frequency
2. Connect Workflow Configuration node with data source parameters
3. Set up Fetch Software Metrics and Fetch BI Dashboard Data nodes with respective API credentials
4. Configure Merge Data Sources node for data consolidation logic
5. Connect Data Validation Agent with OpenAI/Nvidia API credentials for anomaly detection
6. Set up Orchestration Agent with AI API credentials for severity assessment
7. Configure Check for Anomalies node with routing conditions
8. Connect Route by Severity node with classification logic

## How It Works
This workflow automates continuous data integrity monitoring and intelligent alert management across multiple data sources. Designed for data engineers, IT operations teams, and business intelligence analysts, it solves the critical challenge of detecting data anomalies and orchestrating appropriate responses based on severity levels. The system operates on scheduled intervals, fetching data from software metrics APIs and BI dashboards, then merging these sources for comprehensive analysis. It employs AI-powered validation and orchestration agents to detect anomalies, assess severity, and determine optimal response strategies. The workflow intelligently routes alerts based on severity classification, triggering critical notifications via email and Slack for high-priority issues while sending standard reports for routine findings. By maintaining detailed compliance audit logs and preparing executive summaries, it ensures stakeholders receive timely, actionable intelligence while creating audit trails for data quality monitoring initiatives.

## Multi-Source Data Collection
**What:** Fetches and merges data from software metrics APIs and BI dashboard sources on scheduled intervals
**Why:** Ensures comprehensive monitoring coverage by combining operational metrics with business intelligence for complete visibility

## Severity-Based Alert Routing
**What:** Routes alerts through severity-specific channels with tailored messaging for critical, high-priority, and standard notifications
**Why:** Ensures urgent issues receive immediate attention while preventing alert fatigue from routine findings

## AI-Powered Anomaly Detection
**What:** Processes merged data through dual AI agents for validation and intelligent orchestration of anomaly findings
**Why:** Leverages AI to detect subtle patterns and determine appropriate severity classifications beyond simple threshold-based rules

user.name: Cheng Siong Chin
user.bio: Dr. Cheng Siong CHIN is an n8n workflow creator specializing in AI-powered automation, agent orchestration, and intelligent system integrations. He designs and builds end-to-end workflows that combine LLMs, APIs, and data pipelines to streamline complex processes and deliver production-ready automation solutions. Contact me to discuss custom AI workflows and agent architectures.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
